proposition_real: Duty of care arises from voluntary assumption of responsibility in the context of negligent misstatement
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: General duty of care applicable to claims of tortious interference with economic relations
why_incorrect: Hedley Byrne applies to negligent misstatement — not tortious interference; conflating the torts is a fundamental error

proposition_real: Remoteness of damage in contract: only losses in reasonable contemplation of both parties at the time of contracting
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Remoteness of damage principle applicable to tortious claims for expectation damages
why_incorrect: Hadley v Baxendale is a contract remoteness rule; in tort the test is reasonable foreseeability (Wagon Mound)

proposition_real: Parties may exclude liability for any breach if clearly expressed — fundamental breach doctrine rejected
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Doctrine of fundamental breach renders exclusion clauses void for serious breaches
why_incorrect: Photo Production abolished fundamental breach — exclusion clauses construed normally; this misapplication reverses the HL ruling

proposition_real: Remoteness: defendant liable only for losses within scope of assumed contractual responsibility
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Remoteness test in contract is reasonable foreseeability of type of loss
why_incorrect: The Achilleas adds assumption of responsibility on top of Hadley v Baxendale — not a replacement; misapplying it as sole test overstates scope

proposition_real: Contract interpretation: natural and ordinary meaning of words takes priority
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Courts may rewrite contracts where literal interpretation produces uncommercial result
why_incorrect: Arnold v Britton explicitly rejects rewriting — commercial common sense cannot override clear language

proposition_real: Reliance damages: claimant may recover pre-contract expenditure wasted as a result of defendant's breach — does not establish recovery of lost future profits
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Expectation damages: claimant entitled to recover all profits it would have earned had the contract been fully performed
why_incorrect: Anglia TV v Reed established reliance damages for wasted expenditure — not expectation damages for lost profits. Correct authority for expectation damages: Robinson v Harman (1848) 1 Ex 850

proposition_real: Negotiating damages: award calculated by reference to defendant's gain — hypothetical bargain price for release of covenant
proposition_cited_in_skeleton: Expectation damages: court awards damages calculated by reference to the profits the claimant would have earned
why_incorrect: Wrotham Park is gain-based (what the defendant gained) not compensation-based (what the claimant lost). These are opposing measures; conflating them is a fundamental error in quantum
